tools.name: merge_merge_request
tools.description: Merge a merge request

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

merge_request_iid.description: The IID of a merge request

auto_merge.description: If true, the merge request merges when the pipeline succeeds.

merge_when_pipeline_succeeds.description: If true, the merge request merges when the pipeline succeeds.in GitLab 17.11. Use

squash.description: Squash commits into a single commit when merging

tools.name: approve_merge_request
tools.description: Approve a merge request

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

merge_request_iid.description: The IID of the merge request to approve

sha.description: The HEAD of the merge request. Optional, but used to ensure the merge request hasn't changed since you last reviewed it

approval_password.description: Current user's password. Required if 'Require user re-authentication to approve' is enabled in the project settings

tools.name: unapprove_merge_request
tools.description: Unapprove a merge request

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

merge_request_iid.description: The IID of the merge request to unapprove

tools.name: get_merge_request_approval_state
tools.description: Get merge request approval details including approvers

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

merge_request_iid.description: The IID of the merge request

tools.name: get_merge_request_conflicts
tools.description: Get the conflicts of a merge request

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

merge_request_iid.description: The IID of the merge request

tools.name: list_merge_request_pipelines
tools.description: List pipelines for a merge request with pagination

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

merge_request_iid.description: The internal ID of the merge request

per_page.description: Number of items per page (max: 100, default: 20)

tools.name: create_or_update_file
tools.description: Create or update a file in a GitLab project

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

file_path.description: Path where to create/update the file

branch.description: Branch to create/update the file in

previous_path.description: Path of the file to move/rename

commit_id.description: Current file commit ID (for update operations)

tools.name: search_repositories
tools.description: Search for GitLab projects

per_page.description: Number of items per page (max: 100, default: 20)

tools.name: create_repository
tools.description: Create a new GitLab project

tools.name: create_group
tools.description: Create new group or subgroup

parent_id.description: The parent group ID for creating a subgroup

tools.name: get_file_contents
tools.description: Get contents of a file or directory from a GitLab project

project_id.description: Project ID or URL-encoded path (optional; falls back to env)

file_path.description: Path to the file or directory. Takes precedence over 'path' when both are provided

tools.name: push_files
tools.description: Push multiple files in a single commit

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

file_path.description: Path where to create the file

tools.name: create_issue
tools.description: Create a new issue

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

assignee_ids.description: Array of user IDs to assign

issue_type.description: The type of issue. One of issue, incident, test_case or task.

weight.description: Weight of the issue (numeric, typically hours of work)

tools.name: create_merge_request
tools.description: Create a new merge request

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

target_project_id.description: Numeric ID of the target project.

assignee_ids.description: The ID of the users to assign the MR to

reviewer_ids.description: The ID of the users to assign as reviewers of the MR

remove_source_branch.description: Flag indicating if a merge request should remove the source branch when merging.

squash.description: If true, squash all commits into a single commit on merge.

tools.name: fork_repository
tools.description: Fork a project to your account or specified namespace

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

tools.name: create_branch
tools.description: Create a new branch

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

tools.name: get_branch
tools.description: Get branch details (commit, protection status)

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

tools.name: list_branches
tools.description: List branches in project with search filter

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

search.description: Search term to filter branches by name

per_page.description: Number of items per page (max: 100, default: 20)

tools.name: delete_branch
tools.description: Delete branch from project

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

branch_name.description: Name of the branch to delete

tools.name: get_merge_request
tools.description: Get details of a merge request (mergeRequestIid or branchName required)

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

merge_request_iid.description: The IID of a merge request

tools.name: get_merge_request_diffs
tools.description: Get the changes/diffs of a merge request (mergeRequestIid or branchName required)

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

merge_request_iid.description: The IID of a merge request

excluded_file_patterns.description: Array of regex patterns to exclude files from the diff results. Each pattern is a JavaScript-compatible regular expression that matches file paths to ignore. Examples: ["^vendor/", "^test/mocks/", "\.spec\.ts$", "package-lock\.json"]

tools.name: list_merge_request_changed_files
tools.description: List changed file paths in a merge request without diff content (mergeRequestIid or branchName required)

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

merge_request_iid.description: The IID of a merge request

excluded_file_patterns.description: Array of regex patterns to exclude files. Examples: ["^vendor/", "\.pb\.go$"]

tools.name: list_merge_request_diffs
tools.description: List merge request diffs with pagination (mergeRequestIid or branchName required)

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

merge_request_iid.description: The IID of a merge request

per_page.description: Number of items per page (max: 100, default: 20)

unidiff.description: Present diffs in the unified diff format. Default is false. Introduced in GitLab 16.5.

tools.name: get_merge_request_file_diff
tools.description: Get diffs for specific files from a merge request (mergeRequestIid or branchName required)

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

merge_request_iid.description: The IID of a merge request

file_paths.description: List of file paths to retrieve diffs for (e.g. ['src/api/users.ts', 'src/repo/user.go']). Call list_merge_request_changed_files first to get the full list of changed paths.

unidiff.description: Present diff in the unified diff format. Default is false.

tools.name: list_merge_request_versions
tools.description: List all versions of a merge request

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

merge_request_iid.description: The internal ID of the merge request

tools.name: get_merge_request_version
tools.description: Get a specific version of a merge request

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

merge_request_iid.description: The internal ID of the merge request

version_id.description: The ID of the merge request diff version

unidiff.description: Present diffs in the unified diff format. Default is false. Introduced in GitLab 16.5.

tools.name: get_branch_diffs
tools.description: Get diffs between two branches or commits

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

from.description: The base branch or commit SHA to compare from

to.description: The target branch or commit SHA to compare to

straight.description: Comparison method: false for '...' (default), true for '--'

excluded_file_patterns.description: Array of regex patterns to exclude files from the diff results. Each pattern is a JavaScript-compatible regular expression that matches file paths to ignore. Examples: ["^vendor/", "^test/mocks/", "\.spec\.ts$", "package-lock\.json"]

tools.name: update_merge_request
tools.description: Update a merge request (mergeRequestIid or branchName required)

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

merge_request_iid.description: The IID of a merge request

title.description: The title of the merge request

description.description: The description of the merge request

assignee_ids.description: The ID of the users to assign the MR to

reviewer_ids.description: The ID of the users to assign as reviewers of the MR

remove_source_branch.description: Flag indicating if the source branch should be removed

squash.description: Squash commits into a single commit when merging

tools.name: create_note
tools.description: Create a new note (comment) to an issue or merge request

project_id.description: Project ID or namespace/project_path

noteable_iid.description: IID of the issue or merge request

tools.name: create_merge_request_thread
tools.description: Create a new thread on a merge request

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

merge_request_iid.description: The IID of a merge request

base_sha.description: REQUIRED: Base commit SHA in the source branch. Get this from merge request diff_refs.base_sha.

head_sha.description: REQUIRED: SHA referencing HEAD of the source branch. Get this from merge request diff_refs.head_sha.

start_sha.description: REQUIRED: SHA referencing the start commit of the source branch. Get this from merge request diff_refs.start_sha.

position_type.description: REQUIRED: Position type. Use 'text' for code diffs, 'image' for image diffs, 'file' for file-level comments.

new_path.description: File path after changes. REQUIRED for most diff comments. Use same as old_path if file wasn't renamed.

old_path.description: File path before changes. REQUIRED for most diff comments. Use same as new_path if file wasn't renamed.

new_line.description: Line number in modified file (after changes). Use for added lines or context lines. NULL for deleted lines. For single-line comments on new lines.

old_line.description: Line number in original file (before changes). Use for deleted lines or context lines. NULL for added lines. For single-line comments on old lines.

line_code.description: CRITICAL: Line identifier in format '{file_path_sha1_hash}_{old_line_number}_{new_line_number}'. USUALLY REQUIRED for GitLab diff comments despite being optional in schema. Example: 'a1b2c3d4e5f6_10_15'. Get this from GitLab diff API response, never fabricate.

type.description: Line type: 'old' = deleted/original line, 'new' = added/modified line, null = unchanged context. MUST match the line_code format and old_line/new_line values.

old_line.description: Line number in original file (before changes). REQUIRED when type='old', NULL when type='new' (for purely added lines), can be present for context lines.

new_line.description: Line number in modified file (after changes). REQUIRED when type='new', NULL when type='old' (for purely deleted lines), can be present for context lines.

start.description: Start line position for multiline comment range. MUST specify either old_line OR new_line (or both for context), never neither.

line_code.description: CRITICAL: Line identifier in format '{file_path_sha1_hash}_{old_line_number}_{new_line_number}'. USUALLY REQUIRED for GitLab diff comments despite being optional in schema. Example: 'a1b2c3d4e5f6_12_17'. Must be from same file as start.line_code.

type.description: Line type: 'old' = deleted/original line, 'new' = added/modified line, null = unchanged context. SHOULD MATCH start.type for consistent ranges (don't mix old/new types).

old_line.description: Line number in original file (before changes). REQUIRED when type='old', NULL when type='new' (for purely added lines), can be present for context lines. MUST be >= start.old_line if both specified.

new_line.description: Line number in modified file (after changes). REQUIRED when type='new', NULL when type='old' (for purely deleted lines), can be present for context lines. MUST be >= start.new_line if both specified.

end.description: End line position for multiline comment range. MUST specify either old_line OR new_line (or both for context), never neither. Range must be valid (end >= start).

anyOf.description: Line range for multiline comments on GitLab merge request diffs. VALIDATION RULES: 1) line_code is critical for GitLab API success, 2) start/end must have consistent types, 3) line numbers must form valid range, 4) get line_code from GitLab diff API, never generate manually.

line_range.description: MULTILINE COMMENTS: Specify start/end line positions for commenting on multiple lines. Alternative to single old_line/new_line.

width.description: IMAGE DIFFS ONLY: Width of the image (for position_type='image').

height.description: IMAGE DIFFS ONLY: Height of the image (for position_type='image').

x.description: IMAGE DIFFS ONLY: X coordinate on the image (for position_type='image').

y.description: IMAGE DIFFS ONLY: Y coordinate on the image (for position_type='image').

position.description: Position when creating a diff note

created_at.description: Date the thread was created at (ISO 8601 format)

tools.name: resolve_merge_request_thread
tools.description: Resolve a thread on a merge request

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

merge_request_iid.description: The IID of a merge request

merge_request_iid.description: The IID of a merge request

body.description: The content of the draft note

in_reply_to_discussion_id.description: The ID of a discussion the draft note replies to

base_sha.description: REQUIRED: Base commit SHA in the source branch. Get this from merge request diff_refs.base_sha.

head_sha.description: REQUIRED: SHA referencing HEAD of the source branch. Get this from merge request diff_refs.head_sha.

start_sha.description: REQUIRED: SHA referencing the start commit of the source branch. Get this from merge request diff_refs.start_sha.

position_type.description: REQUIRED: Position type. Use 'text' for code diffs, 'image' for image diffs, 'file' for file-level comments.

new_path.description: File path after changes. REQUIRED for most diff comments. Use same as old_path if file wasn't renamed.

old_path.description: File path before changes. REQUIRED for most diff comments. Use same as new_path if file wasn't renamed.

new_line.description: Line number in modified file (after changes). Use for added lines or context lines. NULL for deleted lines. For single-line comments on new lines.

old_line.description: Line number in original file (before changes). Use for deleted lines or context lines. NULL for added lines. For single-line comments on old lines.

line_code.description: CRITICAL: Line identifier in format '{file_path_sha1_hash}_{old_line_number}_{new_line_number}'. USUALLY REQUIRED for GitLab diff comments despite being optional in schema. Example: 'a1b2c3d4e5f6_10_15'. Get this from GitLab diff API response, never fabricate.

type.description: Line type: 'old' = deleted/original line, 'new' = added/modified line, null = unchanged context. MUST match the line_code format and old_line/new_line values.

old_line.description: Line number in original file (before changes). REQUIRED when type='old', NULL when type='new' (for purely added lines), can be present for context lines.

new_line.description: Line number in modified file (after changes). REQUIRED when type='new', NULL when type='old' (for purely deleted lines), can be present for context lines.

start.description: Start line position for multiline comment range. MUST specify either old_line OR new_line (or both for context), never neither.

line_code.description: CRITICAL: Line identifier in format '{file_path_sha1_hash}_{old_line_number}_{new_line_number}'. USUALLY REQUIRED for GitLab diff comments despite being optional in schema. Example: 'a1b2c3d4e5f6_12_17'. Must be from same file as start.line_code.

type.description: Line type: 'old' = deleted/original line, 'new' = added/modified line, null = unchanged context. SHOULD MATCH start.type for consistent ranges (don't mix old/new types).

old_line.description: Line number in original file (before changes). REQUIRED when type='old', NULL when type='new' (for purely added lines), can be present for context lines. MUST be >= start.old_line if both specified.

new_line.description: Line number in modified file (after changes). REQUIRED when type='new', NULL when type='old' (for purely deleted lines), can be present for context lines. MUST be >= start.new_line if both specified.

end.description: End line position for multiline comment range. MUST specify either old_line OR new_line (or both for context), never neither. Range must be valid (end >= start).

anyOf.description: Line range for multiline comments on GitLab merge request diffs. VALIDATION RULES: 1) line_code is critical for GitLab API success, 2) start/end must have consistent types, 3) line numbers must form valid range, 4) get line_code from GitLab diff API, never generate manually.

line_range.description: MULTILINE COMMENTS: Specify start/end line positions for commenting on multiple lines. Alternative to single old_line/new_line.

width.description: IMAGE DIFFS ONLY: Width of the image (for position_type='image').

height.description: IMAGE DIFFS ONLY: Height of the image (for position_type='image').

x.description: IMAGE DIFFS ONLY: X coordinate on the image (for position_type='image').

y.description: IMAGE DIFFS ONLY: Y coordinate on the image (for position_type='image').

tools.name: list_issues
tools.description: List issues (default: created by current user; use scope='all' for all)

project_id.description: Project ID or URL-encoded path (optional - if not provided, lists issues across all accessible projects)

assignee_id.description: Return issues assigned to the given user ID. user id or none or any

assignee_username.description: Return issues assigned to the given username

author_id.description: Return issues created by the given user ID

author_username.description: Return issues created by the given username

created_after.description: Return issues created after the given time

created_before.description: Return issues created before the given time

due_date.description: Return issues that have the due date

issue_type.description: Filter to a given type of issue. One of issue, incident, test_case or task

iteration_id.description: Return issues assigned to the given iteration ID. None returns issues that do not belong to an iteration. Any returns issues that belong to an iteration.

scope.description: Return issues from a specific scope

state.description: Return issues with a specific state

updated_after.description: Return issues updated after the given time

updated_before.description: Return issues updated before the given time

with_labels_details.description: Return more details for each label

per_page.description: Number of items per page (max: 100, default: 20)

tools.name: my_issues
tools.description: List issues assigned to the authenticated user

project_id.description: Project ID or URL-encoded path (optional when GITLAB_PROJECT_ID is set)

state.description: Return issues with a specific state (default: opened)

labels.description: Array of label names to filter by

search.description: Search for specific terms in title and description

created_after.description: Return issues created after the given time (ISO 8601)

created_before.description: Return issues created before the given time (ISO 8601)

updated_after.description: Return issues updated after the given time (ISO 8601)

updated_before.description: Return issues updated before the given time (ISO 8601)

per_page.description: Number of items per page (default: 20, max: 100)

weight.description: Weight of the issue (numeric, typically hours of work)

issue_type.description: The type of issue. One of issue, incident, test_case or task.

tools.name: update_issue_description_patch
tools.description: Apply a patch (search/replace or unified diff) to an issue description. Reduces token usage by allowing small changes without sending the full description. Supports dry_run to preview changes and create_note to summarize updates.

project_id.description: Project ID or URL-encoded path

issue_iid.description: The internal ID of the project issue

patch_type.description: Type of patch format to apply

patch.description: The patch content to apply to the issue description

dry_run.description: If true, preview changes without updating the issue

create_note.description: If true, add a note summarizing the change after update

allow_multiple.description: For search_replace: allow multiple matches to all be replaced (default: false — fail on duplicate)

tools.name: delete_issue
tools.description: Delete an issue

project_id.description: Project ID or URL-encoded path

issue_iid.description: The internal ID of the project issue

tools.name: get_repository_tree
tools.description: List files and directories in a repository

project_id.description: The ID or URL-encoded path of the project

ref.description: The name of a repository branch or tag. Defaults to the default branch.

recursive.description: Boolean value to get a recursive tree

per_page.description: Number of results to show per page

page_token.description: Token for keyset pagination. Use the next_page_token value returned in the previous response to retrieve the next page.

pagination.description: Pagination method. Use 'keyset' for keyset-based pagination (required for repositories with many files). Non-keyset calls keep the legacy array response for backward compatibility; that legacy response shape is deprecated and may be removed in a future major release. Keyset calls return a structured response with items and next_page_token when more pages are available.

tools.name: validate_ci_lint

tools.description: Validate an existing .gitlab-ci.yml configuration for a project

project_id.description: Project ID or URL-encoded path

content_ref.description: Commit SHA, branch, or tag to read the existing CI config from

dry_run_ref.description: Branch or tag context for dry_run validation

include_jobs.description: Include jobs in the lint response

tools.name: list_merge_requests
tools.description: List merge requests (without project_id: user's MRs; with project_id: project MRs)

project_id.description: Project ID or URL-encoded path (optional - if not provided, lists all merge requests the user has access to)

assignee_id.description: Return MRs assigned to the given user ID (integer), 'none', or 'any'. Mutually exclusive with assignee_username.

assignee_username.description: Returns merge requests assigned to the given username. Mutually exclusive with assignee_id.

author_id.description: Returns merge requests created by the given user ID (integer). Mutually exclusive with author_username.

author_username.description: Returns merge requests created by the given username. Mutually exclusive with author_id.

reviewer_id.description: Returns merge requests which have the user as a reviewer. Must be an integer, 'none', or 'any'. Mutually exclusive with reviewer_username.

reviewer_username.description: Returns merge requests which have the user as a reviewer by username. Mutually exclusive with reviewer_id.

approved_by_usernames.description: Returns merge requests approved by the given usernames (array).

created_after.description: Return merge requests created after the given time

created_before.description: Return merge requests created before the given time

updated_after.description: Return merge requests updated after the given time

updated_before.description: Return merge requests updated before the given time

scope.description: Return merge requests from a specific scope

state.description: Return merge requests with a specific state

order_by.description: Return merge requests ordered by the given field

sort.description: Return merge requests sorted in ascending or descending order

target_branch.description: Return merge requests targeting a specific branch

source_branch.description: Return merge requests from a specific source branch

wip.description: Filter merge requests against their wip status

with_labels_details.description: Return more details for each label

per_page.description: Number of items per page (max: 100, default: 20)

tools.description: Get GitLab user details by usernames

usernames.description: Array of usernames to search for

tools.description: Get user details by ID

tools.description: Get current authenticated user details

tools.name: list_commits
tools.description: List repository commits with filtering options

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

ref_name.description: The name of a repository branch, tag or revision range, or if not given the default branch

since.description: Only commits after or on this date are returned in ISO 8601 format YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ

until.description: Only commits before or on this date are returned in ISO 8601 format YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ

all.description: Retrieve every commit from the repository

with_stats.description: Stats about each commit are added to the response

first_parent.description: Follow only the first parent commit upon seeing a merge commit

trailers.description: Parse and include Git trailers for every commit

per_page.description: Number of items per page (max: 100, default: 20)

tools.name: get_commit
tools.description: Get details of a specific commit

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

sha.description: The commit hash or name of a repository branch or tag

tools.name: get_commit_diff
tools.description: Get changes/diffs of a specific commit

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

sha.description: The commit hash or name of a repository branch or tag

full_diff.description: Whether to return the full diff or only first page (default: false)

tools.name: get_file_blame
tools.description: Get git blame for a file at a given ref. Each entry maps a contiguous range of source lines to the commit that last changed them (id, author, authored_date, message). Use range_start/range_end to limit blame to specific lines.

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

file_path.description: The full path of the file to blame, relative to repo root

ref.description: The name of branch, tag or commit (required by GitLab blame API)

range_start.description: First line of the blame range (inclusive, 1-based). Both range[start] and range[end] must be set together.

range_end.description: Last line of the blame range (inclusive, 1-based). Both range[start] and range[end] must be set together.

tools.name: list_commit_statuses
tools.description: List statuses for a commit

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

sha.description: The commit hash or name of a repository branch or tag

name.description: Filter statuses by status name or context

all.description: Return all statuses, not only latest ones

per_page.description: Number of items per page (max: 100, default: 20)

tools.name: create_commit_status
tools.description: Create or update the status of a commit

project_id.description: Project ID or complete URL-encoded path to project

sha.description: The commit hash to set the status on

name.description: Status name. GitLab defaults to 'default' when omitted.

context.description: Alias for name. Provide either name or context, not both.

target_url.description: Target URL associated with this status

coverage.description: Total code coverage for this status

pipeline_id.description: Pipeline ID to attach the status to

tools.name: list_group_iterations
tools.description: List group iterations with filtering options

state.description: Return opened, upcoming, current, closed, or all iterations.

search.description: Return only iterations with a title matching the provided string.

search_in.description: Fields in which fuzzy search should be performed with the query given in the argument search. The available options are title and cadence_title. Default is [title].

include_ancestors.description: Include iterations for group and its ancestors. Defaults to true.

include_descendants.description: Include iterations for group and its descendants. Defaults to false.

updated_before.description: Return only iterations updated before the given datetime. Expected in ISO 8601 format (2019-03-15T08:00:00Z).

updated_after.description: Return only iterations updated after the given datetime. Expected in ISO 8601 format (2019-03-15T08:00:00Z).

per_page.description: Number of items per page (max: 100, default: 20)

tools.description: Upload a file for use in markdown content

project_id.description: Project ID or URL-encoded path of the project

file_path.description: Path to the file to upload

tools.description: Download an uploaded file from a project (images returned as base64; use local_path to save to disk)

project_id.description: Project ID or URL-encoded path of the project

local_path.description: Local path to save the file (optional, defaults to current directory)

tools.name: health_check
tools.description: Verify server status and authentication

tools.name: list_events
tools.description: List events for the authenticated user (before/after: YYYY-MM-DD)

action.description: If defined, returns events with the specified action type

target_type.description: If defined, returns events with the specified target type

before.description: If defined, Returns events created before the specified date (YYYY-MM-DD format). To include events on 2025-08-29, use before=2025-08-30

after.description: If defined, Returns events created after the specified date (YYYY-MM-DD format). To include events on 2025-08-29, use after=2025-08-28

scope.description: Include all events across a user's projects

sort.description: Direction to sort the results by creation date. Default: desc

page.description: Returns the specified results page. Default: 1

per_page.description: Number of results per page. Default: 20

tools.name: get_project_events
tools.description: List events for a project (before/after: YYYY-MM-DD)

project_id.description: Project ID or URL-encoded path

action.description: If defined, returns events with the specified action type

target_type.description: If defined, returns events with the specified target type

before.description: If defined, Returns events created before the specified date (YYYY-MM-DD format). To include events on 2025-08-29, use before=2025-08-30

after.description: If defined, Returns events created after the specified date (YYYY-MM-DD format). To include events on 2025-08-29, use after=2025-08-28

sort.description: Direction to sort the results by creation date. Default: desc

page.description: Returns the specified results page. Default: 1

per_page.description: Number of results per page. Default: 20

tools.name: discover_tools
tools.description: Discover and activate additional tool categories for this session. Available categories: merge_requests, issues, repositories, branches, projects, labels, ci, groups, pipelines, milestones, wiki, releases, tags, users, workitems, webhooks, search. Already-active categories are listed in the response.

category.description: Toolset category to activate (e.g. 'pipelines', 'wiki'). Omit to list available categories.
